text: Our paper "Collaborative Cloud-Edge Computing via LLM-Guided Constrained Reinforcement Learning" was accepted in IEEE International Symposium on Personal, Indoor and Mobile Radio Communications (PIMRC 2026), Singapore. Congratulations Xingqi and Yuhui!

Our paper "Dependency-Aware Task Offloading via Quantum Graph Attention Network-based Deep Reinforcement Learning in Collaborative Edge-Cloud Systems" was accepted in International Conference on Computer Communications and Networks (ICCCN 2026). Congratulations Xingqi and Yuhui!
U.S. provisional patent application filed: "Online Detection and Containment of Disruptive User Equipment (UE) in Radio Access Networks." Inventors: X. Wu and J. Farooq (Application No. 64/080,683).

Our paper "Risk-Aware Resource Orchestration in Multi-Tier 5G Edge-Cloud Networks" was accepted in Intent-Driven Network: Techniques and Applications, Springer Wireless Network Series. Congratulations Xingqi!
Ph.D. researcher Yuhui Wang joins Nokia Bell Labs as a Generative AI Intern for Summer 2026.

Organized the 2nd Workshop on Resilience in Next Generation Networks (NGResNet) at IEEE/IFIP NOMS 2026 in Rome, Italy, with a keynote by Dr. Hans-Peter Bernhard. The lab presented work on proactive PRB allocation for O-RAN industrial networks.
Co-organizing the 5th IEEE CNS Cyber Resilience Workshop (CRW'26) in Newark, Delaware. The call for papers is open with a submission deadline of June 22, 2026.
Yuhui Wang attended the ceremony recognizing Rackham Predoctoral Fellows in Ann Arbor.
Junaid Farooq promoted to Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering with tenure, approved by the U-M Board of Regents.
Our paper "Jamming-Resilient PRB Reservation for Latency-Critical O-RAN Network Slicing" was accepted in IEEE DySPAN 2026 ML-Spec Workshop, Washington D.C., USA. Congratulations Elahe!

Our paper "Temporally Encoded Double DQN for Proactive PRB Allocation in O-RAN Enabled Industrial Networks" was accepted in IEEE/IFIP NOMS 2026 Workshop on Resilience in Next Generation Network Management (NGResNet), Rome, Italy. Congratulations Xingqi and Elahe!
Our paper "Delay-Aware Reinforcement Learning for O-RAN Control under Stochastic Reward Feedback" was accepted in IEEE Networking Letters. Congratulations Xingqi!
Yuhui Wang received his Rackham Predoctoral Fellowship certificate from Rackham Dean Dewey Jung, recognizing his research on aerial communication systems.
Yuhui Wang and Xingqi Wu received pins at the CECS Doctoral Pinning and Recognition Ceremony, celebrating their advancement to candidacy.
Lab members presented their research posters at the CECS Research Poster Competition.
Our paper "Process-Informed Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Joint Radio-Compute Orchestration in Industrial O-RAN" was accepted in IEEE Networking Letters. Congratulations to Xingqi and Yuhui!

Chaired the Dearborn Automation and Intelligent Systems (DAISY) Symposium, featuring a panel on NextG networks and smart manufacturing with Majid Butt, Haneya Naeem Qureshi, and Georges Ayoub. Supported by the UM Bold Challenges Pollination Award.
The CyReN Lab and Elahe Delavari featured in UM-Dearborn's Campus in Focus: March Maize-ness.
Elahe Delavari participated in the Three Minute Thesis (3MT) competition at the University of Michigan-Dearborn.
Invited talk at Grand Valley State University: "Enabling Assured Autonomy: AI-Driven Optimization and Reliability in Next-Generation Networks."

Our paper "PROMPTGUARD: Zero Trust Prompting for Securing LLM-Driven O-RAN Control" was accepted in Proc. NDSS 2026 Workshop on Security and Privacy of Next-Generation Networks (FutureG), San Diego CA. Congratulations Xingqi and Yuhui!

Our paper "Dynamic Multi-Modal UAV Control for Optimized Coverage and Backhaul Connectivity in Spatially Unstructured and Dispersed User Environments" was accepted in IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing. Congratulations Yuhui!
New book published: Cyber Resilience in Next-Generation Networks (Foundations and Trends in Networking).

Our paper "SliceWise: Traffic-Aware Multi-Agent RL with Temporal Encoding for Joint Slice Admission and Resource Orchestration in O-RAN" was accepted in IEEE Networking Letters. Congratulations Xingqi and Yuhui!

Our paper "Resilient PRB Allocation for High Mobility UEs in 5G O-RAN via LSTM-Enhanced MPC" was accepted in IEEE Globecom Workshop on Resilience in Next-Generation Wireless Communication Networks (GC Wkshps 2025), Taiwan. Congratulations Xingqi and Yuxuan!
Our paper "Multi-agent Resource Orchestration Based on D3QN for Network Slicing in 5G Edge-Cloud Networks" was accepted in IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management. Congratulations Xingqi!
Hosted Prof. Waheed Bajwa at the University of Michigan-Dearborn for a seminar and discussions with faculty and students.

Our paper "Joint Positioning and Computation Offloading in Multi-UAV MEC for Low Latency Applications: A Proximal Policy Optimization Approach" was accepted in IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing. Congratulations Yuhui!
Our paper "Poster: Triage Slicing: Online Detection and Containment of Disruptive UEs in O-RAN Slices" was accepted in 26th ACM MobiHoc 2025, Houston, TX, USA. Congratulations Xingqi and Ritesh!
Poster accepted at ACM MobiHoc 2025. Congratulations Xingqi!
Our paper "Evaluation of LLM powered agentic AI for solving multi-arm bandit problems" was accepted in IEEE COINS 2025, Madison WI, USA. Congratulations Jawad!
Our paper "Multimodal LLM-guided sequential detection of cyber threats in electric vehicle charging systems" was accepted in IEEE COINS 2025, Madison WI, USA. Congratulations Ritesh!

Our paper "Real-time multiple obstacle avoidance and navigation in unstructured spaces with applications to indoor delivery robots" was accepted in IEEE COINS 2025, Madison WI, USA. Congratulations Yuhui!
Our paper "Reliable and Resilient Connectivity and Coverage under Localized Backhauling in UAV-IoT Networks" was accepted in IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society. Congratulations Xingqi!
Presented two papers from the group at IEEE ICC 2025 in Montreal, on URLLC admission control in O-RAN and UAV-assisted LEO satellite edge computing, with Xingqi Wu and Yuhui Wang.
Paper accepted in IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society. Congratulations Xingqi!

Our paper "Coordinated UAV Deployment and Task Offloading in UAV-Assisted LEO Satellite Edge Computing Systems via Proximal Policy Optimization" was accepted in IEEE ICC 2025 Workshop on Emerging Technologies in Aerial and Space Networks, Montreal, Canada. Congratulations Xingqi and Yuhui!

Our paper "Joint Admission Control and Resource Provisioning for URLLC Traffic in O-RAN: A Constrained Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Approach" was accepted in IEEE ICC 2025, Montreal, Canada. Congratulations Xingqi!
Organized the inaugural Workshop on Resilience in Next Generation Networks (NGResNet) at IEEE INFOCOM 2025 in London, with a keynote by Prof. Ness Shroff.
Attended the NSF Athena AI Institute Summit at Duke University, with discussions on edge computing, AI, and intelligent systems.

Our paper "LLM-Driven Agentic AI Approach to Enhanced O-RAN Resilience in Next-Generation Networks" was accepted in IEEE INFOCOM 2025 Workshop on Resilience in Next-Generation Networks (NGResNet), London, UK. Congratulations Xingqi and Yuhui!

Our paper "LLM-Powered Agentic AI Approach to Securing EV Charging Systems Against Cyber Threats" was accepted in 3rd Workshop on Cybersecurity of Critical National Infrastructures (CCNI), IEEE WoWMoM 2025, Dallas TX, USA. Congratulations Ritesh!
Presented two papers from the group at IEEE WCNC 2025 in Milan, on multi-UAV placement with LLM-driven optimization and computation offloading in multi-UAV MEC networks, with Yuhui Wang.
Our paper "Joint Optimization of Positioning and Computation Offloading in Multi-UAV MEC Networks for Low Latency Applications" was accepted in IEEE WCNC 2025, Milan, Italy. Congratulations Yuhui!
Our paper "Multi-UAV Placement for Integrated Access and Backhauling Using LLM-Based Optimizers" was accepted in IEEE WCNC 2025, Milan, Italy. Congratulations Yuhui!
Presented LLM-xApp, a large language model framework for adaptive radio resource management in O-RAN, at the NDSS 2025 FutureG Workshop in San Diego, with Xingqi Wu and Yuhui Wang.
Junaid Farooq elevated to Senior Member of the IEEE.
Presented a seminar at UM-Flint on AI-Driven Management and Orchestration of Next-Generation Networks.

Our paper "LLM-xApp: A Large Language Model Empowered Radio Resource Management xApp for 5G O-RAN" was accepted in Proc. NDSS 2025 Workshop on Security and Privacy of Next-Generation Networks (FutureG), San Diego CA. Congratulations Xingqi and Yuhui!
Our paper "Mapping Cyber Threats in the 5G Supply Chain: Landscape, Vulnerabilities, and Risk Management" was accepted in IEEE Network. Congratulations Moyan!
Helped organize a two-day NSF Workshop on Large Language Models for Network Security at New York University.
Co-organized the 2nd Cyber Resilience Workshop at IEEE CNS 2023 in Orlando, FL.
Delivered the Ph.D. commencement address at the NYU Tandon School of Engineering.
UM-Dearborn news feature on the NSF Convergence Accelerator project for zero-trust 5G security in ultra-sensitive applications.
Multi-university NSF project on Zero Trust cybersecurity for 5G networks kicks off.
